steps.title: Identify the key assertion
steps.content: Text 1 warns that conjugation can turn harmless nanomaterials into dangerous ones.

steps.title: Apply Text 2's response
steps.content:
Text 2 explicitly acknowledges the toxicity concern “has drawn deserved attention,” validating Text 1's warning.

But the example of silicon dioxide and zinc oxide—which actually reduced DNA damage risk—proves this negative outcome is not inevitable.

The other choices either deny the risk exists, overstate the benefits argument, or wrongly claim researchers underestimated complexity.

That matches Choice D — By agreeing that the possibility described in Text 1 is a cause for concern but pointing out that nanomaterial conjugation does not inevitably produce that result.
